stack.backend: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Edge Functions + Realtime + Auth + Storage)

always: Use @supabase/ssr — createBrowserClient for Client Components, createServerClient for Server Components/Route Handlers
Enable RLS on every table before writing any query
Call supabase.auth.getUser() in middleware.ts to refresh the session cookie on every request
Use timestamptz for all timestamps — never timestamp without timezone
Generate types after every schema change: supabase gen types typescript --linked > src/types/supabase.ts
Use text + CHECK constraint for status columns — never Postgres enum (hard to migrate)
Store Supabase service role key only in Edge Functions and server-only code — never expose to client
never: Never import from @supabase/supabase-js directly in Server Components — always use @supabase/ssr
Never use the old @supabase/auth-helpers-nextjs package — it is deprecated, use @supabase/ssr
Never call cookies() outside of Server Components or Route Handlers — it throws in client code
Never store auth tokens in localStorage — Supabase SSR handles cookie-based sessions automatically
Never skip RLS on a new table — enable it at creation time, not as an afterthought
Never use getServerSideProps or getStaticProps — App Router uses Server Components instead
Never expose SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY as a NEXT_PUBLIC_ variable
gotchas: createServerClient requires you to pass cookies() from next/headers — call it inside the function body, not at module level (cookies() throws if called at import time)
middleware.ts must call supabase.auth.getUser() (not getSession()) and update the response cookies — if you skip this, the session expires silently and SSR pages see the user as logged out
Server Components cannot set cookies — if you need to update the session in a Server Component, do it in middleware instead
Route Handlers need await cookies() in Next.js 15+ — the cookies() API became async
Supabase Realtime subscriptions must be set up in Client Components with useEffect — they cannot run server-side
RLS policies using auth.uid() require the Supabase client to be initialized with the user's JWT — the service role client bypasses RLS entirely
supabase.storage.from().getPublicUrl() returns a URL even for private buckets — it does NOT check if the user has access
Next.js rewrites in next.config.js do not affect Edge Middleware — middleware.ts runs before rewrites
Supabase Edge Functions cold start is ~150ms — not suitable for paths called on every page load
TypeScript: supabase.from<Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Row']>() is verbose — generate a typed helper: createClient<Database>()

folders.app/: Next.js App Router — page.tsx = route, layout.tsx = layout
folders.app/(auth)/: route group for auth pages (login, signup, reset)
folders.app/api/: Route Handlers — thin, call service functions
folders.components/: shared UI components — Client Components marked with 'use client'
folders.lib/supabase/server.ts: createServerClient — import in Server Components and Route Handlers
folders.lib/supabase/client.ts: createBrowserClient — import in Client Components only
folders.middleware.ts: session refresh on every request — required for SSR auth to work
folders.types/supabase.ts: auto-generated Supabase types — never edit manually
folders.supabase/functions/: Deno Edge Functions — use service role for privileged writes
